sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

questions.passage: Researchers studied red blood cells from patients carrying hemoglobin S (HbS). Oxygen saturation curves for HbA and HbS were similar at high PO2, but HbS-containing cells became sickled as PO2 fell. A compound that stabilized oxygenated HbS reduced sickling in low-oxygen chambers without changing total hemoglobin concentration.
questions.stem: Why does sickling increase as PO2 decreases?

choices.A: Deoxygenated HbS polymerizes more readily than oxygenated HbS
choices.B: Low PO2 destroys all heme groups irreversibly
choices.D: Red blood cells begin transcribing beta-globin under hypoxia

explanations.A: Correct. The figure shows sickling rises as oxygen falls, consistent with deoxygenated HbS polymerization.
explanations.B: Hemoglobin still binds oxygen; heme groups are not all destroyed.
explanations.C: Hemoglobin is a tetramer with quaternary structure.
explanations.D: Mature red blood cells lack nuclei and do not transcribe beta-globin.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Oxygen saturation and sickling as PO2 changes.

questions.stem: The compound that stabilizes oxygenated HbS would most likely:

choices.C: Shift HbS toward a high-affinity relaxed state
choices.A: Increase the number of nuclei in erythrocytes

explanations.C: Correct. Stabilizing the oxygenated state favors the R state, reducing deoxygenated HbS polymer formation.
explanations.B: Heme requires iron for oxygen binding.
explanations.D: Hemoglobin normally displays cooperative binding; the passage does not suggest eliminating it.

choices.B: A hydrophobic valine replaces a charged glutamate in beta-globin
choices.A: A cysteine replaces every histidine in alpha-globin
choices.C: A lysine is inserted into heme itself
choices.D: A stop codon removes all globin chains

explanations.B: Correct. HbS results from a glutamate-to-valine substitution in beta-globin, increasing hydrophobic interactions when deoxygenated.
explanations.A: This is not the classic HbS mutation.
explanations.C: Heme is a prosthetic group, not a polypeptide sequence containing lysine.
explanations.D: A complete absence of globin chains would not produce HbS tetramers.

questions.stem: Which physiological region would most promote sickling?

choices.D: Systemic capillaries with relatively low PO2
choices.B: Pulmonary veins immediately after oxygen loading
choices.C: Arterial blood leaving the left ventricle

explanations.D: Correct. Sickling rises at low PO2, which is more characteristic of peripheral tissues extracting oxygen.
explanations.A: Alveoli are high oxygen environments that favor oxygen loading.
explanations.C: Arterial blood leaving the left heart is relatively oxygenated.

questions.stem: A patient with chronic hemolysis would most directly increase production of which cell lineage to compensate?

explanations.B: Correct. Loss of red blood cells stimulates erythropoiesis from erythroid precursors, largely under erythropoietin control.
explanations.A: Osteoclasts resorb bone and do not replace red blood cells.

questions.passage: Neutrophils were placed in a chamber containing buffer, complement fragment C5a, opsonized bacteria, or C5a with a receptor-blocking antibody. C5a increased directed migration, while opsonized bacteria increased phagocytosis. Blocking the C5a receptor reduced migration toward C5a but did not prevent phagocytosis of opsonized bacteria in separate wells.
questions.stem: Which function of C5a is best supported by the figure?

choices.B: Direct digestion of bacterial cell walls

explanations.A: Correct. C5a markedly increases migration index, supporting its role as a neutrophil chemoattractant.
explanations.B: The assay measures migration and phagocytosis, not direct enzymatic digestion by C5a.
explanations.C: Plasma cell antibody production is not the measured neutrophil response.
explanations.D: C5a is a complement fragment, not an oxygen carrier.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 2. Neutrophil migration and phagocytosis assays.

questions.stem: Opsonized bacteria most directly promote phagocytosis because they:

choices.C: Are coated with molecules recognized by phagocyte receptors
choices.D: Cannot be recognized by innate immune cells

explanations.C: Correct. Opsonins such as antibodies or complement fragments tag microbes for receptor-mediated engulfment.
explanations.A: Opsonization adds recognizable surface tags; it does not remove all antigens.
explanations.B: Hemoglobin binding is irrelevant to this neutrophil assay.

questions.stem: Blocking the C5a receptor reduces migration but not phagocytosis of opsonized bacteria because:

choices.D: Chemotaxis and opsonin-mediated engulfment use distinct receptor systems
choices.A: C5a is the only opsonin in serum

explanations.D: Correct. The data separate C5a-driven chemotaxis from opsonin receptor-mediated phagocytosis.
explanations.A: C5a is a chemoattractant; opsonins include IgG and C3b.
explanations.B: Neutrophils have many receptors, including complement and Fc receptors.
explanations.C: Hemoglobin polymerization is unrelated to neutrophil phagocytosis.

questions.stem: Which leukocyte is most directly represented in this passage?

explanations.A: Correct. The passage explicitly studies neutrophils, innate immune cells specialized for chemotaxis and phagocytosis.
explanations.B: Erythrocytes transport oxygen and lack nuclei.

questions.stem: Which result would best show that C5a receptor signaling is sufficient to orient neutrophil movement?

choices.C: A C5a gradient causes directional migration in the absence of bacteria
choices.A: Opsonized bacteria increase phagocytosis in every condition
choices.B: Hemoglobin saturation is unchanged by complement
choices.D: Drug treatment lowers all cell viability to zero

explanations.C: Correct. Directional migration toward a C5a gradient without bacteria would isolate C5a receptor signaling as sufficient for chemotaxis.
explanations.A: This tests phagocytosis, not directional chemotaxis.
explanations.B: Hemoglobin saturation is unrelated to neutrophil orientation.
explanations.D: Killing cells prevents interpretation of signaling sufficiency.
